title: Retrospective Validation Prespecified Protocol — Human-Only Thresholds
description: Evidence Foundry E1 Phase 4 (FR-24, OQ-5, ADR-0006). TOOL-LOCAL BY DESIGN, same posture as fixture-corpus.schema.json (tools/retro-validate/schemas/, not schemas/, so no wave-2 phase touches scripts/validate-kb.mjs). Shapes the ONE kind of prespecified validation protocol document `report --protocol <path>` may ever accept: every threshold-bearing field below is `const: null` -- structurally TBD-by-named-humans. This is not a placeholder default that happens to be null today; there is no value other than `null` that satisfies this schema for any threshold field, in Evidence Foundry E1. A document that declares a real (non-null) dangerous-miss rate, utility-measure, or per-stratum threshold FAILS this schema and is rejected fail-closed by `lib/protocol.mjs#assertProtocolShape` before `report` computes or writes anything. Populating a real threshold, and rendering any report software-qualifying against one, are human governance acts entirely outside this schema and this tool (see `lib/metrics.mjs#evaluateProtocolQualification`, which independently never returns `qualifying: true` regardless of this schema's own gate). Passing this schema is a STRUCTURAL claim only -- it never certifies clinical validity, statistical power, IRB/DUA compliance, or that any named human has actually reviewed or pinned real values (this schema cannot, and does not try to, verify that `authoredBy` entries are real people).

schemaVersion.description: REQUIRED, non-null. Integer protocol-document-shape version, bumped only when this schema's own shape changes. The one metadata field `lib/metrics.mjs#findPopulatedProtocolFields` allowlists as not counting toward a document's own "populated" detection.

protocolId.description: Stable slug identifying this protocol document (e.g. for future run-provenance cross-reference). Never a patient identifier.

description.description: Free-text human summary of this protocol's intended scope (e.g. which module/candidate it will eventually apply to). Must never declare, imply, or approximate a real threshold value in prose -- that would defeat the structural `const: null` guarantee below by smuggling a number into free text; this field is not, and cannot be, schema-checked against that.

authoredBy.description: REQUIRED, non-empty. The named human(s) who own this protocol document -- FR-24's "TBD-by-named-humans" is an authorship requirement, not merely a null-threshold requirement: a protocol with no named human owner still cannot ever be treated as prespecified by anyone. This schema cannot verify these are real people; it only enforces the shape.

namedHumanAuthor.description: One named human protocol owner/author. Never a synthetic or software-generated identity in a real (non-fixture) protocol document -- this schema cannot enforce that distinction structurally, so it remains a human/governance obligation, exactly like `sourceAttestation` in fixture-corpus.schema.json.

thresholds.description: REQUIRED. Every leaf threshold value under this object is `const: null` in Evidence Foundry E1 -- software never invents or defaults a clinical threshold. A future, out-of-repo human governance act (gate G3 and beyond, never a task in this plan) is the only way any of these fields could ever carry a real value, and even then, honoring it against a report is a human verdict, never something this tool or schema performs.

dangerousMissRateThreshold.description: The maximum acceptable dangerous-miss rate a future human-authored protocol would pin. `const: null` in E1: TBD-by-named-humans, never software-set.

utilityMeasures.description: REQUIRED. Slots for the clinical-utility thresholds a future human-authored protocol would pin (never computed, claimed, or defaulted by this tool -- see `lib/metrics.mjs`'s own header for where the line between software-agreement measures and these human-only thresholds sits). Every field is `const: null` in E1.

strata.description: REQUIRED object naming the three stratification axes a future human-authored protocol may define per-stratum thresholds against (subgroup, analyzer/instrument, and originating site). Each axis is an array (possibly empty in a not-yet-fully-specified protocol) of `$defs/stratumThreshold` entries -- every entry's own threshold field is `const: null`, exactly like the top-level thresholds above.

subgroup.description: Patient-subgroup strata (e.g. an age band or clinical presentation group a future protocol would stratify on).

analyzer.description: Analyzer/instrument strata (e.g. a specific hematology analyzer model a future protocol would stratify on).

site.description: Originating clinical-site strata a future protocol would stratify on.

stratumThreshold.description: One named stratum within a `strata` axis, carrying its own `const: null` dangerous-miss-rate threshold slot -- a per-stratum threshold is exactly as human-only as the top-level one.

stratumName.description: Human-readable name of this stratum (e.g. "age 6-24mo", "Sysmex XN-1000", "site-b"). Never a patient identifier.

dangerousMissRateThreshold.description: `const: null` in E1: TBD-by-named-humans for this specific stratum.
